diseases.name: Apple Scab
diseases.symptoms: Dark, olive-green to black velvety spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves may curl, distort, and drop early.
diseases.treatment: Apply fungicide sprays (captan, myclobutanil) starting at green tip stage. Remove fallen leaves in autumn.
diseases.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure good air circulation by proper pruning.

diseases.name: Apple Black Rot
diseases.symptoms: Frogeye leaf spots with purple margins. Black, rotting lesions on fruit that enlarge concentrically.
diseases.treatment: Prune out dead wood and mummified fruits. Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl fungicides.
diseases.prevention: Remove all sources of inoculum. Maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization.

diseases.name: Cedar Apple Rust
diseases.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Tube-like structures on leaf undersides.
diseases.treatment: Apply fungicides (myclobutanil, triadimefon) at pink bud stage through petal fall.
diseases.prevention: Remove nearby cedar and juniper trees. Plant resistant apple varieties.

diseases.name: Tomato Early Blight
diseases.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings (target-like pattern) on lower leaves first, progressing upward.
diseases.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or copper-based fungicides. Remove and destroy infected leaves.
diseases.prevention: Rotate crops. Mulch around plants. Avoid overhead watering. Stake plants for airflow.

diseases.name: Tomato Late Blight
diseases.symptoms: Large, irregularly shaped, water-soaked lesions on leaves. White fuzzy growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions.
diseases.treatment: Apply metalaxyl or mancozeb immediately. Remove and destroy all infected plants.
diseases.prevention: Use certified disease-free transplants. Ensure good drainage. Avoid overhead irrigation.

diseases.name: Tomato Leaf Mold
diseases.symptoms: Pale green to yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Olive-green to grayish-purple fuzzy mold on undersides.
diseases.treatment: Improve greenhouse ventilation. Apply chlorothalonil or copper fungicides.
diseases.prevention: Reduce humidity. Space plants adequately. Use resistant varieties.

diseases.name: Tomato Bacterial Spot
diseases.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves, stems, and fruit. Spots may have yellow halos.
diseases.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides. Remove severely infected plants.
diseases.prevention: Use disease-free seeds and transplants. Avoid working with wet plants. Rotate crops.

diseases.name: Corn Common Rust
diseases.symptoms: Small, circular to elongate, cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces.
diseases.treatment: Apply foliar fungicides (triazoles, strobilurins) if detected before tasseling.
diseases.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Early planting to avoid peak spore periods.

diseases.name: Corn Northern Leaf Blight
diseases.symptoms: Long, elliptical, grayish-green to tan lesions (1-6 inches) on leaves.
diseases.treatment: Apply foliar fungicides at early signs. Use strobilurin or triazole fungicides.
diseases.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate away from corn for at least one year. Till residues.

diseases.name: Grape Black Rot
diseases.symptoms: Reddish-brown circular lesions on leaves. Fruit turns brown, shrivels into hard black mummies.
diseases.treatment: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb from bud break through veraison.
diseases.prevention: Remove mummified berries and infected canes. Ensure good canopy management.

diseases.name: Potato Early Blight
diseases.symptoms: Dark brown, concentric ringed spots on older leaves. Tubers may show dark, sunken lesions.
diseases.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicides on a 7-10 day schedule.
diseases.prevention: Rotate crops for 2-3 years. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain adequate fertility.

diseases.name: Potato Late Blight
diseases.symptoms: Water-soaked, pale green lesions that rapidly enlarge to dark brown/black. White mold on undersides.
diseases.treatment: Apply metalaxyl + mancozeb or dimethomorph immediately. Destroy infected plants.
diseases.prevention: Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes. Destroy volunteer plants and cull piles.

diseases.name: Rice Blast
diseases.symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions with gray centers and brown borders on leaves. Neck rot at panicle base.
diseases.treatment: Apply tricyclazole or isoprothiolane fungicides. Drain fields temporarily.
diseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Maintain balanced nutrition.

diseases.name: Wheat Stripe Rust
diseases.symptoms: Yellow-orange pustules arranged in stripes along leaf veins. Severe infection causes leaf desiccation.
diseases.treatment: Apply triazole or strobilurin fungicides at first sign of infection.
diseases.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Eliminate volunteer wheat. Adjust sowing dates.

diseases.name: Healthy Plant
diseases.symptoms: No visible symptoms of disease. Plant appears vigorous with normal coloration.
diseases.treatment: No treatment required. Continue regular maintenance.
diseases.prevention: Maintain proper watering, fertilization, and pest management schedules.
